title: Setting up Bitwarden Lite on Proxmox LXC

content.text: As a first project I decided to set up a password manager. In my life I used mostly two: Keepass and Bitwarden. I heard that the second one is fairly easy to configure so I decided to go for it (just fyi, there wasn’t a huge research behind it). Also, I wanted to do it in a little bit more complicated way than just using Vaultwarden script from Proxmox Scripts, but at the same time I don’t really know what the best practices are so please have some mercy on me :P. Lately Bitwarden released Lite version for personal projects which was a huge win for my budget setup (Thinkcentre M700 Tiny). My plan was to create an LXC container with Debian 13, 8GB of storage, 512MB of memory and leave 1 core, but as the docs say it should be enough for my usage (I set it for like 4 times at this point, so this config also turned out in practice). Following Bitwarden documentation I also installed Docker Engine to run the container (in a container, that’s what makes my setup sus for me). Guide for the Bitwarden Lite installation:
https://bitwarden.com/help/install-and-deploy-lite/#tab-docker-run-5JDM3sqPaeOnRiPXhlVjSX
After creating container I had to fix not working network adapter:

ping 8.8.8.8
ping: connect: Network is unreachable
ip a
Noticed that the adapter is down, so I turned it on:
ip link set eth0 up
And activated DHCP for the little guy:
dhclient eth0

ping wp.pl
PING wp.pl (212.77.98.9) 56(84) bytes of data. 
64 bytes from www.wp.pl (212.77.98.9): icmp_seq=1 ttl=60 time=8.30 ms
64 bytes from www.wp.pl (212.77.98.9): icmp_seq=2 ttl=60 time=7.90 ms
64 bytes from www.wp.pl (212.77.98.9): icmp_seq=3 ttl=60 time=8.47 ms
Now with the working network adapter we can move to configuring new user so we won’t stay on the root all the time (safety yay)
apt update
apt install sudo
adduser sperek
usermod -aG sudo sperek
su sperek
Escape root dir + sudo check at the same time
sudo cd ../home/sperek
Now installing docker engine from their docs:

# Add Docker's official GPG key:
sudo apt update
sudo apt install ca-certificates curl
sudo install -m 0755 -d /etc/apt/keyrings
sudo curl -fsSL https://download.docker.com/linux/debian/gpg -o /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc
sudo chmod a+r /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc

# Add the repository to Apt sources: 
sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/docker.sources <<EOF
Types: deb
URIs: https://download.docker.com/linux/debian
Suites: $(. /etc/os-release && echo "$VERSION_CODENAME") 
Components: stable
Signed-By: /etc/apt/keyrings/docker.asc
EOF
sudo apt update
sudo apt install docker-ce docker-ce-cli containerd.io docker-buildx-plugin docker-compose-plugin
And check if the docker is running fine:

systemctl status docker
* docker.service - Docker Application Container Engine
     Loaded: loaded (/usr/lib/systemd/system/docker.service; enabled; preset: enabled)
     Active: active (running) since Thu 2026-03-26 11:51:36 UTC; 42s ago
Now we can move to the Bitwarden part. First, I created settings.env file in the user directory:
nano settings.env
I used this basic config from the Bitwarden and changed few things later:

#####################
# Required Settings #
#####################

# Server hostname
BW_DOMAIN=bitwarden.example.com

# Database
# Available providers are sqlserver, postgresql, mysql/mariadb, or sqlite
BW_DB_PROVIDER=mysql
BW_DB_SERVER=db
BW_DB_DATABASE=bitwarden_vault
BW_DB_USERNAME=bitwarden
BW_DB_PASSWORD=super_strong_password

# Installation information
# Get your ID and key from https://bitwarden.com/host/
BW_INSTALLATION_ID=00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000
BW_INSTALLATION_KEY=xxxxxxxxxxxx
Then I generated ID and KEY from https://bitwarden.com/host/. Lastly, I ran the docker container:
sudo docker run -d --name bitwarden -v /$(pwd)/bwdata/:/etc/bitwarden -p 80:8080  --env-file settings.env ghcr.io/bitwarden/lite
It worked!

sudo docker ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE                    COMMAND            CREATED              STATUS              PORTS                                  NAMES 
7d6a813c69a7   ghcr.io/bitwarden/lite   "/entrypoint.sh"   About a minute ago   Up About a minute   0.0.0.0:80->8080/tcp, [::]:80->8080/tcp   bitwarden

Getting into the website using eth0 ip address and http (got me confused for a while, turned out that Firefox was constantly putting that “s” at the end). Despite working frontend, the website could not be loaded. I just watched the wheel spinning and spinning…
In the docker logs:
docker logs bitwarden
I found an this:
2026-03-26 12:36:06,968 WARN exited: admin (terminated by SIGABRT (core dumped); not expected)

At the first glance I thought that it might be something connected to the LXC not having full access to kernel to use cryptography tools so I tweaked these settings: keyctl for “kernel keyring” access and FUSE for AppImage (that was Gemini’s idea). After that I removed container to run it again:
docker rm -f bitwarden
Unfortunately it didn’t fix my issue which made me give up for few days. Upon return I decided to check browser console and noticed this error:
Unhandled error in angular Error: Could not instantiate WebCryptoFunctionService. Could not locate Subtle crypto.
Which brought me to this thread:
https://github.com/dani-garcia/vaultwarden/discussions/5486
I decided to use a tailscale HTTPs for now, as I have it configured for my pve.
https://tailscale.com/docs/features/containers/lxc/lxc-unprivileged
I installed it and then generated a certificate using:
tailscale cert <domain>
I also had to enable proxy for bitwarden container to be accessible:
sudo tailscale serve –bg http://127.0.0.1:80
WE FINALLY GOT IT LETS GO!!

Maybe in the future I will try to use Cloudflare with my domain which for now is only used for this website. (Also I have to upgrade the network adapter configuration as it resets after every container restart…)
UPDATE: I assumed it was working because of login screen, however I can't log in getting 500 error, tbh I'm thinking about moving to Vaultwarden at this point xd

UPDATE: Tried installing Vaultwarden and it worked perfectly fine so I have no idea what went wrong there... Also, when creating LXC I forgot to tick the DHCP box so now interface works out of the box :D
